# Generated by Forge — do not edit manually

# cursor-hackaton

web app on port 3001.

- API base URL: `http://localhost:3001/api`
- API docs: `http://localhost:3001/docs`
- OpenAPI JSON: `http://localhost:3001/openapi.json`

## Environment

Review `.env` values (defaults are copied from `.env.example` at scaffold):

| Variable | Required | Default | Description |
|----------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `API_DOMAIN` | no | `localhost` | — |
| `APP_NAME` | no | `Forge App` | — |
| `BETTER_AUTH_SECRET` | yes | `change-me-in-production-min-32-chars-long` | — |
| `DATABASE_URL` | yes | `postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/app` | PostgreSQL connection string |
| `EMAIL_FROM` | yes | `noreply@example.com` | Default sender address for outbound email |
| `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` | yes | `your-google-client-id` | — |
| `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET` | yes | `your-google-client-secret` | — |
| `LAST_LOGIN_METHOD_COOKIE_NAME` | no | `last-login-method` | — |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_DOMAIN` | no | `localhost` | Root domain for cross-subdomain cookies (leading dot added automatically) |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_LAST_LOGIN_METHOD_COOKIE_NAME` | no | `last-login-method` | Cookie name for last login method (must match server) |
| `NEXT_PUBLIC_WEB_URL` | yes | `http://localhost:3001` | Public URL of this web app (Better Auth client baseURL) |
| `ORIGINS` | no | `http://localhost:3001` | — |
| `SMTP_HOST` | yes | `localhost` | — |
| `SMTP_PASS` | no | — | — |
| `SMTP_PORT` | yes | `1025` | — |
| `SMTP_SECURE` | no | `false` | — |
| `SMTP_USER` | no | — | — |
| `WEB_URL` | yes | `http://localhost:3001` | — |

## Infrastructure

Start the required services from the project root:

```bash
docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up postgres mailpit -d
```

- PostgreSQL: `localhost:5432` (`DATABASE_URL` defaults to local Postgres)
- Mailpit SMTP: `localhost:1025`
- Mailpit UI: `http://localhost:8025`

## Setup

Run these commands from this app directory in order:

```bash
pnpm install
```

### Auth-server

**Apply auth schema to Prisma**

```bash
pnpm exec better-auth generate --config src/lib/auth.ts --output prisma/schema.prisma --yes
```

**Run migrations after auth schema**

```bash
pnpm exec prisma migrate dev
```

## Run

```bash
pnpm dev
```

- Health check: `http://localhost:3001/api/health/so` (Bearer `SERVICE_ACCESS_TOKEN`)

## Observability

Built-in Sparktech observability is included in every app:

- `pnpm bootstrap` — validates `SERVICE_*` variables and runs connectivity checks
- `GET /api/health/so` — detailed health payload (Bearer `SERVICE_ACCESS_TOKEN`)

```bash
curl -sS -H "Authorization: Bearer $SERVICE_ACCESS_TOKEN" http://localhost:3001/api/health/so
```

Optional structured logging: `forge add logging` (Pino + Better Stack Logtail for nextjs).

## Modules

### Auth-client

Better Auth React client with organization, 2FA, magic link, and related plugins.

Import `authClient` from `@/lib/auth-client` or `@/providers/auth-provider`. Pair with `auth-server` for a full Next.js auth stack, or point at an external API (e.g. `api:auth`).

### Tanstack

TanStack ecosystem packages with a shared QueryClient provider.

Use generated Kubb hooks or import from `@tanstack/react-query`, `@tanstack/react-table`, and related packages directly.

### Auth-server

Better Auth on Next.js with full UI at /auth/*, SaaS dashboard at /dashboard/*, and API at /api/auth/*.

Sign in at /auth/sign-in. After login you are redirected to /dashboard. Use getSession() from @/lib/auth-session in server components.

## Useful commands

- `pnpm seed` — tsx prisma/seeds/dev.ts
- `pnpm seed:staging` — tsx prisma/seeds/staging.ts
- `pnpm seed:prod` — tsx prisma/seeds/prod.ts
- `pnpm email:dev` — email dev --dir src/templates/emails
